commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: graceful shutdown to prevent DNS resolution issues

The sidecar was being force-killed (SIGKILL) on app exit, which left
orphaned utun interfaces with dead IPv6 routes and could leave the
macOS remoted daemon suspended. Both cause DNS resolution failures
that persist for hours after the app closes.

Changes:
- Add centralized cleanup module (src/cleanup.py) with signal handlers,
  atexit hooks, and startup cleanup for crash recovery
- Track utun interfaces created by pymobiledevice3 tunnels so we can
  clean them up on exit
- Rust exit handler now sends SIGTERM first, waits up to 5s for Python
  to gracefully tear down tunnels, falls back to SIGKILL
- Fallback cleanup always runs: brings down orphaned utun interfaces
  with fd-prefix IPv6 addresses and resumes remoted if suspended
- Fix sidecar binary path resolution for production builds (Contents/MacOS/)
- Fix shell.sidecar() key to match externalBin config
- dev.sh uses same graceful shutdown pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: parse generic arrow functions (<T>() => body) in expression position

When encountering '<' in unary expression parsing, use lookAhead to
detect generic arrow function pattern: <TypeParams>(Params) [: RetType] =>
If detected, route to parseArrowFunction instead of parseTypeAssertion.
This fixes cases like <T, X>() => { ... } being misparsed as
comparison/comma expressions.

fix(ci): prevent gate job from being skipped on upstream failure (#797)

When a needed job fails, GitHub Actions skips dependent jobs by default.
This means `all-checks-passed` gets a "skipped" status instead of
"failure", which can bypass branch protection rules that require it to
pass. Use `if: always()` and explicitly check the result to ensure the
gate job always reports a clear pass/fail.

commit.message:
feat: continue agent sessions across max-turns retries via git branch

When a Claude Code pod hits --max-turns and exits, the emptyDir workspace
is destroyed and any uncommitted work is lost. This change makes retries
resumable by using git as a checkpoint.

- Add Task.PriorBranchName field so the engine knows a prior attempt
  pushed work to a recoverable branch
- Update BuildPrompt to always prescribe a deterministic branch name
  (robodev/<ticket-id>), instruct the agent to commit and push
  frequently, and include branch_name in result.json
- Add a ## Continuation section to the prompt when PriorBranchName is
  set, cloning that branch with --depth=50 and asking the agent to
  review prior commits before continuing
- Set task.PriorBranchName in launchRetryJob from tr.Result.BranchName
  when available, falling back to robodev/<ticketID> for retries where
  the pod was killed before the stop hook wrote result.json
- Add tests for all new prompt and controller behaviour

fix(extrator-json): resolve UniqueViolation no slug de extraction_variables

O diff de criação era calculado por categoria_id, mas o unique index
ix_extraction_variables_slug é global. Quando duas categorias tinham
o mesmo slug (ex: decisoes_resumo_decisao), o INSERT falhava com
duplicate key.

Agora, antes de criar, verifica se o slug já existe globalmente.
Se existir, prefixa com o namespace da categoria (ex: detran_decisoes_...).
Fallback com sufixo numérico quando não há namespace.
A variável original permanece intacta.

Arquivos: services_json_sync.py, router_ext_models.py

commit.message:
fix: autopilot→fsd switch while paused now clears pause and restarts loop

When the 3-way toggle cycles manual→autopilot→fsd, the autopilot phase
starts a safeguarded loop. If that loop pauses (e.g. no-progress) before
the user clicks again to reach fsd, switching autopilot→fsd previously
did nothing — the pause reason stayed and no new loop was started.

Now detects autopilot↔fsd transitions while paused, clears pauseReason,
and enqueues a fresh loop in the new mode. Running loops still pick up
mode changes dynamically (no restart needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(client): resolve type drift from regenerated OpenAPI types (#99)

The checked-in client types had drifted from the live OpenAPI spec:
- audit-utils: schema names renamed (AuditLogDto, AuthAuditEntryDto)
- api-client: custom fetch signature for openapi-fetch strict mode
- GlobalSearchDialog/ReceiptItemForm: unwrap paginated response shape
- ReceiptItemsCard: make receiptItemCode/subcategory optional
- Test files: add mustResetPassword to JwtPayload literals

These only surfaced in Docker builds which regenerate types from the
API at build time, while CI used stale checked-in types.

commit.message:
feat: mobile UX — hidden bottom sheet with menu button + onboarding arrow fixes (#46)

## Summary
- Bottom sheet now hides completely on mobile (was showing 120px peek);
opened via a menu button next to the search bar
- Menu button uses hamburger icon (more accurate than funnel since sheet
contains filters + trip + auth)
- Onboarding arrow (first-visit overlay) now co-located with the menu
button as a flex-col child — structurally guaranteed to point directly
at the button without pixel offsets
- Arrow uses same two-path black-outline style as the desktop arrow
- `showOnboarding` derived boolean extracted to avoid duplicating the
condition

## Test plan
- [ ] Mobile: bottom sheet not visible on load
- [ ] Mobile: tap menu button → sheet slides up
- [ ] Mobile: first visit (clear localStorage) → arrow bounces directly
below menu button
- [ ] Desktop: unchanged (sidebar, desktop overlay arrow still works)
- [ ] Badge on menu button appears when categories are selected

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Fix Phosphor Icons: switch from web components to CSS class API

The previous commit used custom element syntax (<ph-x />, <ph-chat-circle />)
but @phosphor-icons/web provides a CSS icon font, not web components. Those
elements were never defined so all icons rendered empty.

Fixes:
- Replace all ph-* custom elements with <i class="ph-* ph-icon-name"> syntax
- Fix CSS selector from custom element tags to i[class*="ph-"]
- Replace getStatusIcon() emoji (✅🔄⬜⏭️) with Phosphor icons:
  done→ph-check-circle (fill), in-progress→ph-circle-notch (bold),
  ready→ph-circle (regular), skipped→ph-skip-forward (fill)

commit.message:
Retry when model produces only thinking with no visible output

With adaptive thinking, the model may use all output tokens on
thinking and produce no text or tool_use blocks. Previously this
caused the agent to silently end with no response. Now it nudges
the model to continue and produce actual output.

commit.message:
Keep session alive when switching offices via dropdown

Removed Auth.clearSession() from switchOffice() so admins don't
have to re-login when switching between offices. If their email
isn't in the new office roster, the login screen shows automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-agent reactions — Architect and Spark respond to Scribe in real time

- Add reactionEngine.js: pure timer-based engine with probability rules, special moment detection, cross-reactions, and cleanup
- Special moments: Problem (both react), Done (Spark 95% / Arch 40%), Decision (both immediately, 5s stagger), Elegant (both)
- Normal flow: Architect 20% (+25% on arch keywords), Spark 30% (+25% on done keywords), never both
- Cross-reactions: Spark→Architect (35%), Architect→Spark (20%), one cross-reaction max per exchange
- 8–18s reaction delay, 5–10s cross-reaction delay; user keydown cancels all pending timeouts
- API routes: accept reactionPrompt field, inject into system prompt, cap max_tokens at 300 for reactions
- dashboard: triggerReaction function, reaction engine init/destroy, Focus mode (10-min timer, cancels all pending)
- ChatMessage: isReaction visual treatment — ↩ label, 0.9rem font, left indent, muted opacity
- LatticeDrawer: Focus button in handle bar, ◉ Focus indicator when active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add three-tier CORS handling and admin config (#129) (#130)

Scope FlowCorsFilter to /api/v1/flow/** only, add DiscoveryCorsFilter
with wildcard CORS for /.well-known/**, and add AdminCorsFilter with
configurable CORS for /api/v1/admin/** (restricted to urls.root when
integrated, wildcard when external). Introduce AdminConfig sealed class
with admin.enabled and admin.integrated-ui properties. Print admin UI
URL at startup when integrated UI is enabled.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): bypass pydantic-settings JSON parsing for list[str] fields

pydantic-settings 2.2.x JSON-parses list[str] fields BEFORE field_validators
run. Plain strings like 'RS256' or 'https://fitprogress.ovh' fail json.loads()
and raise SettingsError immediately, so validators never fire.

Fix: add _CommaOrJsonEnvSource that overrides decode_complex_value to fall back
to the raw string on JSON failure. Existing field_validators (mode="before")
for CORS_ORIGINS and AUTH0_ALGORITHMS then receive the raw string and handle
both comma-separated and JSON-array formats.

Also improve parse_cors_origins validator to match parse_algorithms: supports
JSON arrays, comma-separated strings, and empty values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Start timeline at 1947 and auto-play on scroll into view

Timeline scrubber now initializes at the beginning (1947) instead of
the latest entry, and automatically plays through history when the
component scrolls into view via IntersectionObserver. Play button
remains functional for manual replay.
